summary.name: Cross-Asset Time-Series Momentum Volatility-Weighted
summary.author: Dimitar Dimitrov
summary.description: The strategy is a cross asset time series momentum and diversification overlay that allocates to the strongest performing liquid ETF exposures and risk balances them by recent realized volatility. summary.description_full:
The strategy is a cross asset time series momentum and diversification overlay that allocates to the strongest performing liquid ETF exposures and risk balances them by recent realized volatility. Each month it computes a composite momentum score for each candidate ETF using total return price performance over approximately 1, 3, 6, and 12 month lookbacks (about 21 trading days per month), averages these horizons to reduce dependence on any single window, and ranks the universe. The portfolio then holds the top 5 ETFs by this combined momentum signal, seeking to capture persistent return continuation across major risk premia and global asset classes.

The investable universe is a fixed set of US listed ETFs spanning US equities (large cap, small cap, Nasdaq), developed and emerging equities, REITs, investment grade credit, gold, and US Treasuries across the curve plus aggregate bonds. Rebalancing occurs at each month end using daily data, after market close, with weights set proportional to inverse 3 month realized volatility (computed from daily log returns), producing an equal risk contribution style allocation among the selected winners. Risk control is primarily via diversification, monthly turnover discipline, and volatility scaling; positions not in the top 5 are fully exited at rebalance.

raw.name: Cross-Asset Time-Series Momentum Volatility-Weighted
raw.description:
The strategy is a cross asset time series momentum and diversification overlay that allocates to the strongest performing liquid ETF exposures and risk balances them by recent realized volatility. Each month it computes a composite momentum score for each candidate ETF using total return price performance over approximately 1, 3, 6, and 12 month lookbacks (about 21 trading days per month), averages these horizons to reduce dependence on any single window, and ranks the universe. The portfolio then holds the top 5 ETFs by this combined momentum signal, seeking to capture persistent return continuation across major risk premia and global asset classes.

The investable universe is a fixed set of US listed ETFs spanning US equities (large cap, small cap, Nasdaq), developed and emerging equities, REITs, investment grade credit, gold, and US Treasuries across the curve plus aggregate bonds. Rebalancing occurs at each month end using daily data, after market close, with weights set proportional to inverse 3 month realized volatility (computed from daily log returns), producing an equal risk contribution style allocation among the selected winners. Risk control is primarily via diversification, monthly turnover discipline, and volatility scaling; positions not in the top 5 are fully exited at rebalance.
